description: A plain candid operational photograph (no on-image text or graphic design) taken on a sunny day at a waterfront dock. A gray and orange U.S. Coast Guard response boat with 'U.S. COAST GUARD' painted on its hull is moored alongside a low floating pontoon dock; a small American flag flies from the boat. Several uniformed personnel (some in dark uniforms, some in operational gear) stand on the boat and dock area. Behind the boat are green trees, wooden dock pilings, and a white house on the far shore, with calm blue water in the foreground. This appears to relate to Nantucket/Martha's Vineyard enforcement operations mentioned in the tweet. There is no slogan, seal overlay, or meme styling, so it is documentary rather than a designed graphic.
notable_text: U.S. COAST GUARD (painted on the boat hull)
